title: Imageless imagery in aphantasia revealed by early visual cortex decoding.

assets.abstract: Activity in the early visual cortex is thought to tightly couple with conscious experience, including feedback-driven mental imagery. However, in aphantasia (a complete lack of visual imagery), the state of mental imagery, what takes its place, or how any activity relates to qualia remains unknown. This study analyzed univariate (amplitude) and multivariate (decoding) blood-oxygen-level-dependent (BOLD) signals in primary visual cortex during imagery attempts. "Imagery" content could be decoded

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Mental imagery — visual imagery as real perception, imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.
